PBL · Scenario 1
Data-Driven Content Strategy  ·  StreamKUS Original Series

From Audience Comments
to Content Strategy Text Mining Analysis of Netflix Hit Series

Combining 2,388 YouTube viewer comments across 16 Netflix series with a supplementary Rotten Tomatoes corpus of 3,013 reviews — building an evidence-based content blueprint for StreamKUS's first original releases.

01 · Introduction

Moving Beyond Intuition

StreamKUS, a rising media company, is preparing to launch its very first original content tailored for YouTube and Netflix. Historically, the content strategy team relied entirely on vague intuition to plan new shows, which frequently yielded disappointing results. In contrast, competitors who systematically study viewer reactions have consistently produced successful content.

This report presents a data-driven approach conducted by the content data analysis team at StreamKUS. By collecting viewer comments and reactions to existing popular content, we leveraged text mining techniques to uncover the specific genres, themes, and story elements that truly resonate with our target audience. Our primary data source is YouTube viewer comments gathered via the YouTube Data API. To further validate these findings, we additionally reference a Rotten Tomatoes corpus of 3,013 audience reviews spanning Netflix Global Top 10 titles (Feb 1 – May 3, 2026).

The two sources are complementary rather than competing: YouTube comments reveal what specific series audiences actively talk about in real-time, while the Rotten Tomatoes data shows what broader structural qualities separate high-rated from low-rated Netflix content overall.
02 · Data Collection

Two Sources, One Strategy

To capture authentic audience behavior, this project selected proven hit Netflix series that maintained a position in the top viewership rankings for consecutive weeks within a 90-day window — including Virgin River, The Lincoln Lawyer, The Night Agent, and Alpha Males.

Obtaining raw, unfiltered audience reactions proved to be a critical yet challenging task. Initially, we considered Reddit.com, but the API key issuance process was excessively complex. Platforms like TMDB imposed severe technical restrictions on comment extraction. Consequently, our team utilized the Google Cloud Console to acquire a YouTube Data API key and built a custom script to directly scrape authentic viewer comments from official Netflix trailers and related videos.

▲ Official Netflix trailer pages used as YouTube comment collection targets
▲ Multiple trailers selected per series to maximize comment volume and diversity

After collecting 100–200 target comments per video, we iteratively refined our pipeline within Codex and Google Colab to filter out noise — removing ultra-short comments, emoji-only posts, and duplicates to curate a high-purity dataset.

▲ Raw viewer comments collected from YouTube — before cleaning process
▲ High-purity comment dataset after rigorous noise removal

Supplementary Source: Rotten Tomatoes Corpus

As an external validation layer, we additionally referenced a Rotten Tomatoes audience review corpus covering 3,013 reviews across 18 Netflix Global Top 10 titles (Feb 1 – May 3, 2026), analyzed through text mining, sentiment analysis, and word frequency mapping.

Rotten Tomatoes data provides a platform-independent view of what drives high vs. low audience ratings — complementing our YouTube comment analysis, which captures real-time fan engagement signals for specific series.
03 · Text Mining Methods

A Five-Step Analytical Pipeline

The core of this project lies in our text mining pipeline, engineered to extract latent consumer needs from unstructured text data. Running extensive testing and optimization cycles within Codex and Google Colab, we established the following rigorous five-step pipeline:

1
Preprocessing Data Preprocessing

Removed special characters, URLs, and redundant whitespaces, then tokenized sentences into distinct word units. Eliminated standard stop words and normalized the text to maximize analytical accuracy.

2
Keyword Frequency Analysis Keyword Frequency Analysis

Extracted the most frequently occurring words and two-word phrases from refined text. This metric provides a direct window into the elements that instantly capture viewer attention.

3
Keyword Classification & Sentiment Analysis Keyword Classification & Sentiment Analysis

Categorized keywords into 'Viewer Sentiment/Praise' (e.g., good, love, great) and 'Content Elements' (e.g., alpha, characters, chemistry) — isolating why viewers appreciated a show, not just that they liked it.

4
TF-IDF Analysis TF-IDF (Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency)

Applied TF-IDF to isolate keywords unique to individual titles, filtering out generic terms shared across all shows — mapping the core genre signals and distinctive themes driving each success story.

5
Evidence Sampling & Topic Clustering Evidence Sampling & Topic Clustering

Sampled the top 30 comments ordered by Like Count to capture the qualitative context of audience engagement, illustrating the narratives — romance arcs, action sequences — that triggered massive community agreement.

04 · Findings

What the Data Actually Reveals

Our YouTube text mining pipeline uncovered three core findings. Each is further contextualized by the Rotten Tomatoes corpus, which independently confirms the same structural patterns at a broader scale.

01

The Virality of Black Comedy Rooted in Social Satire

Analysis of Alpha Males (YouTube, 115 comments) — Viewers respond explosively to comedy that satirizes modern internet memes and contemporary definitions of masculinity, rather than simple slapstick. TF-IDF and frequency analysis pinpointed the exact cultural triggers driving this engagement.

funny · freq 9
Top content-element keyword
alpha · TF-IDF 0.217
Highest distinctive keyword
andrew tate · TF-IDF 0.135
Contemporary meme reference
sigma · TF-IDF 0.109
Satirical culture terminology
alphafunnyandrew tate sigmamalesbritishlaughinglove
Alpha Males · Most-Liked Comment
"Can't believe Andrew Tate can play so many distinct characters."
♥ 383 likes — sharp social commentary deeply engages target audience
Alpha Males
"I swear this is the funniest thing I've watched this year, enjoying every episode."
♥ 227 likes
Alpha Males — Keyword Frequency
Alpha Males — TF-IDF Score
▲ Keyword distribution
▲ TF-IDF output
▲ Top-liked comment evidence
Rotten Tomatoes Validation

The RT corpus independently confirms this signal: among high-rated Netflix content (4–5 stars), unique character dynamics and addictive, emotionally resonant comedy consistently appear as top-performing keywords. Conversely, low-rated content clusters around "predictable" and "boring" — underscoring that generic comedy without a distinct satirical edge fails to retain audiences.

02

Visual Healing and Character Chemistry as Audience Lock-In

Analysis of Virgin River (YouTube, 200 comments) — The sentiment keyword 'love' (freq: 55) appeared at a disproportionately higher rate than any other title. Viewers seek emotional refuge in picturesque environments and wholesome character bonds — not high-stress conflict.

love · freq 55
Dominant sentiment — far above other titles
river · TF-IDF 0.276
Setting attachment — strongest distinctive term
mel · TF-IDF 0.276
Protagonist emotional attachment
beautiful · freq 11
Visual comfort keyword
lovemeljackriver beautifulscenerychemistryvirgin river
Virgin River · Most-Liked Comment
"No craziness, no excessive drama... Just real emotions and mature love. If you love a serene, comforting series, this is perfect!"
♥ 1,370 likes
Virgin River
"Is it just me or this TV show seems to be using the exact same scenery as 'The Hart of Dixie'?"
♥ 1,586 likes — scenery & setting attachment evidenced
Virgin River — Keyword Frequency
Virgin River — TF-IDF Score
Rotten Tomatoes Validation

The RT corpus directly echoes this finding: "emotional" and "chemistry" are two of the most statistically prominent keywords in high-rating (4–5 star) Netflix reviews. This cross-platform alignment strongly validates that emotional comfort and character chemistry are not niche preferences — they are primary drivers of audience satisfaction across platforms and viewer types.

03

High-Stakes Suspense and a Resilient Protagonist Drive Binge-Watching

Analysis of The Night Agent & The Lincoln Lawyer (YouTube, 192 + 196 comments) — In thriller and legal drama formats, immersion and binge-watching potential are paramount. A compelling cliffhanger structure combined with an underdog protagonist battling through adversity is vital for retention.

The Night Agent

action · freq 13
Core genre signal
jason bourne · TF-IDF 0.121
Audience's genre reference point
agent · TF-IDF 0.173
Series identity keyword
actionagentnightbournebinge

lawyer · TF-IDF 0.278
#1 genre identity keyword
mickey · freq 10
Protagonist emotional attachment
intense · content keyword
Suspense & tension signal
lawyermickeyintensebookbinge
The Night Agent · Most-Liked
"Just watched this and I could not stop — watched all 10 episodes in one go. Honestly the most addicting show I've ever watched."
♥ 3,944 likes — highest single like count in our dataset
The Lincoln Lawyer
"Hoping season 4 will be another masterpiece as the first 3."
♥ 592 likes — strong franchise & binge demand signal
The Night Agent — Keyword Frequency
The Lincoln Lawyer — Keyword Frequency
Rotten Tomatoes Validation

The RT corpus provides the clearest validation of this finding: "action", "addictive", and "cliffhanger" rank among the most prominent keywords in high-rating reviews — while "predictable", "boring", and "slow" dominate low-rating feedback. This pattern, drawn from 3,013 cross-platform reviews, confirms that tight pacing and engineered suspense are not just preferences of YouTube commenters but near-universal markers of Netflix content success.

Rotten Tomatoes: Broader Corpus Context

The following corpus-level analysis from Rotten Tomatoes (Feb 1 – May 3, 2026) covers 3,013 audience reviews across 18 Netflix Global Top 10 titles, analyzed through text mining, sentiment indexing, and word frequency mapping. It provides an independent, platform-agnostic view of what separates successful Netflix content from underperforming titles.

05 · Content Proposals

Three Data-Backed Original Series

Grounded strictly in the insights from Section 4 — and reinforced by the Rotten Tomatoes corpus — we propose three tailored original content concepts for StreamKUS's launch:

A
Finding 01 · Alpha Males + RT "addictive characters" data

"The Alpha Syndrome" — Hyper-Realistic Satirical Comedy

Data Evidence: Massive engagement surrounding parodies of contemporary social tropes (alpha TF-IDF: 0.217, andrew tate: 0.135, funny: freq 9). RT corpus confirms "distinct characters" and "addictive" quality are top high-rating drivers.

Strategic Direction: A 20-minute mid-form sitcom following an influencer protagonist who presents a flawless 'alpha' persona online but constantly succumbs to hilarious, unglamorous real-world crises.

Distinct, caricature-driven personalities will generate bite-sized moments optimized for YouTube Shorts and TikTok, sparking organic viewer-driven viral marketing.

Social SatireBlack Comedy20-min Mid-FormViral-First
B
Finding 02 · Virgin River + RT "emotional chemistry" data

"Maple Ridge" — Premium Visual-Healing Romance

Data Evidence: Visual beauty and low-stress relationship dynamics (love: freq 55, river TF-IDF: 0.276). RT corpus independently validates "emotional" and "chemistry" as leading high-rating keywords.

Strategic Direction: A slow-paced drama following a burnt-out urban professional who relocates to a breathtaking forest village called 'Maple Ridge'. Cinematic camera work, rich color grading, and organic human warmth — minimal melodrama.

Offers an emotional sanctuary for exhausted modern viewers, fostering deep attachment and securing a stable, highly loyal fandom.

Healing RomanceScenic SettingCharacter ChemistryFandom Loyalty
C
Finding 03 · Night Agent + Lincoln Lawyer + RT "cliffhanger / addictive" data

"Operation: Shadow Pact" — Non-Stop Espionage & Legal Thriller

Data Evidence: Strong consumer demand for fast-paced action (action: freq 13), legal tension (lawyer TF-IDF: 0.278), and binge-inducing arcs. RT corpus shows "action", "addictive", and "cliffhanger" dominate high-rating reviews; "predictable" and "slow" define failures.

Strategic Direction: A high-stakes thriller centered on a brilliant, deeply flawed defense attorney dragged into a corporate-state conspiracy — combining psychological showdowns with kinetic action sequences.

Every episode closes on a punishing cliffhanger. Relentless action combined with underdog-vs-institution stakes makes this StreamKUS's flagship tentpole franchise.

Action ThrillerLegal DramaCliffhanger StructureTentpole Franchise
06 · Conclusion

A Unified Picture from Two Data Sources

This project set out to replace intuition-based content planning with a rigorous, data-driven framework. Taken together, our YouTube comment analysis and the Rotten Tomatoes corpus tell a coherent and mutually reinforcing story about what makes Netflix content succeed — and what causes it to fail.

Our YouTube findings revealed three clear audience engagement patterns: viewers of Alpha Males respond powerfully to sharp social satire and meme-infused comedy; Virgin River audiences lock in through emotional comfort, visual beauty, and character chemistry; and The Night Agent and The Lincoln Lawyer fans are driven by relentless suspense, strong protagonists, and binge-worthy episode structures. These are not vague preferences — they are quantifiable signals, confirmed by keyword frequency counts and TF-IDF scores extracted from 2,388 real viewer comments.

The Rotten Tomatoes corpus — 3,013 reviews across 18 Netflix Global Top 10 titles — independently converges on the same conclusions. High-rated content clusters around action, emotional resonance, strong characters, addictive pacing, and cliffhangers. Low-rated content clusters around predictability, slow pacing, clichéd plotting, and poor character development. What is particularly striking is that these cross-platform signals align with near-perfect precision: "emotional" and "chemistry" appear in both Virgin River's YouTube comments and the RT high-rating word cloud; "action", "addictive", and "cliffhanger" appear in both The Night Agent's YouTube data and RT's success markers.

This cross-platform alignment is not coincidental — it reflects genuine, platform-independent audience psychology. When two entirely different data sources, collected through different methods and from different viewer populations, point to the same structural qualities, those qualities can be treated as reliable strategic inputs rather than data artifacts.
🎭
Character-Driven Lock-In

Named protagonists dominate YouTube keyword lists; "characters" tops RT high-rating reviews. Strong leads are non-negotiable.

❤️
Emotional Resonance

"Love" and "emotional" appear across both datasets. Emotional connection sustains long-term viewership regardless of platform.

⚡
Engineered Suspense

"Addictive", "cliffhanger", "couldn't stop" — binge-worthy architecture must be designed deliberately, not improvised.

🎯
Clear Genre Identity

Genre terms (action, lawyer, alpha) appear as top TF-IDF signals. Audiences need an immediate, legible identity promise.

🚫
Avoid Predictability

RT low-rating corpus: "predictable", "boring", "cliché" are the clearest failure signals. Generic execution is the primary risk.

📊
Data Integrity First

115 curated Alpha Males comments outperformed raw volume. Quality of data — not quantity — enabled the depth of insight.

As students approaching data crawling and text mining for the first time, establishing the YouTube API pipeline and navigating unstructured natural language preprocessing presented a steep learning curve. But watching meaningful consumer insights emerge from thousands of raw text entries — and seeing those insights independently validated by a completely separate data source — made the process as rewarding as it was rigorous.

The pipeline built during this project provides an invaluable foundation. By institutionalizing this data-driven methodology, StreamKUS can confidently mitigate production risks and systematically maximize the success rate of its future original releases.
